content.full_text:
A

k

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 15, 1989

Schelley Kurle
Western Elections
Post Office Box 8900
Stockton, CA 95208

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-099

Dear Ms. Kurle:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of Grass Valley City Administrator/City Clerk David A.
Breninger regarding application of the mass mailing provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act") to the publication of Grass
Valley's sample ballot.1/

QUESTION

You have included a proposed sample ballot booklet with your
letter. Is the use of the elected city administrator/city clerk's
name in the Grass Valley sample ballot booklet permitted under the
mass mailing restriction of Proposition 73?

CONCLUSION

In the proposed sample ballot booklet you included with your
letter, the elected city administrator/city clerk's name may not
appear except under limited circumstances. Use of the elected
city administrator/city clerk's name in the Grass Valley sample
ballot booklet is permitted only where the name is included in a
return address on the standard forms or envelopes of the agency,
or wherever the name is required by law.

1/ Government Code Section 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Schelley Kurle
March 15, 1989
Page 2


FACTS

Western Elections is preparing to print a sample ballot
booklet to be mailed to more than 200 persons in a calendar month
in anticipation of the Grass Valley general municipal elections.
The proposed sample ballot booklet includes the name of the Grass
Valley city administrator/city clerk in a variety of places. You
have informed us that the city administrator/city clerk is an
elected officer of the city.

On the front cover of the booklet appears the following
statement:

Compiled and Distributed by
DAVID A. BRENINGER
City Administrator/City Clerk

On two of the inside pages, Mr. Breninger's name appears with the
address of the Grass Valley City Hall for correspondence purposes.
The first directs inquiries concerning the election to
Mr. Breninger at city hall, and the second discusses the procedure
for obtaining an absentee ballot, again directing inquiries to
Mr. Breninger at city hall.

In addition, on the inside back cover Mr. Breninger's name
appears with the address of the Grass Valley City Hall on an
absentee ballot application as part of the return address.
Finally, Mr. Breninger's name appears on the back cover with the
return address of the Grass Valley City Hall, and, again, in a
notice regarding the procedure for returning the absentee ballot.

ANALYSIS

A mass mailing is defined in Section 82041.5 as more than two
hundred substantially similar pieces of mail. Government Code
Section 89001 provides that no newsletter or other mass mailing
shall be sent at public expense. Read together, these two
sections could be construed to ban all mailings of 200 or more
substantially similar documents if the mailings are done at public
expense. However, the Commission has adopted Regulation 18901
(copy enclosed)27/ which sets out specific exceptions to the
prohibition on mass mailings.


27/ This regulation although adopted by the Commission at its
December 6, 1988, meeting is under review with the Office of
Administrative Law. We anticipate that it will be in effect by
April 1, 1989. We are advising that the regulation be followed
while it is undergoing that review.

Schelley Kurle
March 15, 1989
Page 3


Regulation 18901(c) provides that a newsletter or other mass
mailing may not be sent at public expense if the name, photograph
or signature of the elected officer appears on the document, or if
it is designated as being from the elected officer or his or her
office, and the elected officer exercises direction and control
over the content, production, or distribution of the document.

However, Regulation 18901(e) carves out an exception to this
prohibition when the name of the elected official appears solely
on the standard letterhead, forms or envelopes of the official's
agency.

This exception permits inclusion of the name of the elected
official in the standard forms and the return address of the
envelopes of the agency. An absentee ballot application
constitutes a form for the purposes of this exception. Thus, if
the absentee ballot application which includes the name of the
city administrator/city clerk is the standard form used by the
agency in previous elections, the inclusion of his name is permit-
ted on the form.

The exception in Regulation 18901(e) is limited to the inclu-
sion of the elected official's name on the standard letterhead,
forms and envelopes of the agency and not in any text. The mass
mailing restrictions prohibit designation of the mailing as being
from the elected official, and the exceptions in the regulation
are to be narrowly construed. Thus, absent use of the name in the
standard letterhead, forms or envelopes of the agency, any refer-
ence to the elected official in the body of the document is
prohibited unless it meets the requirements of another exception.

The standard form exception and all the other provisions of
Regulation 18901 are to be narrowly construed in light of the
language of Section 89001. With this in mind we conclude that the
sample ballot booklet which includes the name of the city
administrator/city clerk in several places is not a form for the
purposes of Regulation 18901(e). Thus, unless the use of the city
administrator/city clerk's name in the mass mailing falls within
some other exception enumerated in Regulation 18901, his name is
prohibited from appearing in the document.


The Legal Notice Exception

Regulation 18901(f)(6) provides an exception for mailings in
which the inclusion of an elected official's name is required by
law, court order or order adopted by an administrative agency.
This exception permits use of the official's name, office, title
or signature where it is expressly required by law to be included
in the mailing. Thus, if the use of the name, office, title or
signature is required by statute, ordinance, charter provision or

Schelley Kurle
March 15, 1989
Page 4


administrative regulation, it may be included in the sample ballot
booklet. (Riddle Advice Letter, No. A-88-311, copy enclosed.)

     Use of the name of the elected city clerk in the sample ballot
booklet is, therefore, permitted despite Section 89001 only if
it is required by law. Absent evidence of such a requirement, we
conclude that reference to the city administrator/city clerk in
the text or on the cover of the sample ballot booklet is
prohibited by Section 89001.

     If I can be of any further assistance to you, please feel
free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By: John W. Wallace
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure

K/P GRAPHICS
WESTERN ELECTIONS
Post Office Box 8900
Stockton, California 95208
(209) 465-5614

February 8, 1989


California Fair Political Practices Commission
Attention: Legal Division
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

To Whom It May Concern:

     Due to the recent passage of Proposition 73, we would like to be
advised of the policies for the enclosed pages of an upcoming Sample
Ballot Booklet.

     I have highlighted the areas in question and have indicated what
type of page each name is occurring upon.  Please let us know if these
regulations will be the same for all of the future pages which will
occur in Sample Ballot Booklets so that we may continue to utilize the
correct standards.

     If you have any questions on the materials presented or need
further explanation of our concerns, please contact either Dwight
Gentry or Schelley Kurle at (209) 465-5614.

Thank You

Schelley Kurle
Production Assistant


Enc.

ARE YOU INTERESTED IN SERVING ON A
FUTURE PRECINCT ELECTION BOARD?

Would you like to serve on a precinct election board on Election Day at
some future election? The Elections Department of the Nevada County Clerk's
Office welcomes applicants. Write to the Elections Department, County Clerk's
Office, 201 Church St., Nevada City, CA 95959 to let them know of your
interest. You will be contacted as vacancies occur.

job no 25067

file no. 185-AV-2
1-3-89
dh

Unsick PAGE

Absentee Voting Information

If you find that for any reason you will be unable to vote in person on election day, promptly complete and sign the enclosed application for absent voter's ballot and mail in stamped envelope or return in person to David A. Breninger, City Administrator/City Clerk, Grass Valley City Hall, 125 East Main Street, Grass Valley, California 95945 - Telephone (916) 273-2203.

Any registered voter who wishes to vote by absent voter's ballot may do so by applying in writing between 29 days before the election (April 3, 1989) and 7 days before the election (April 25, 1989). The application must be signed by the voter and should include the voter's residence address and the address to which the absent voter's ballot is to be sent if it differs from the voter's residence address. A separate application must be submitted by each voter. An application form is provided for your convenience in this voter pamphlet.

Beginning April 26, 1989 and until Election Day, May 2, 1989, any voter unable to go to the polls because of illness or disability resulting in his or her confinement in a hospital, sanatorium, nursing home, or place of residence, or any voter who because of physical handicap is unable to go to his or her polling place or gain access to it due to architectural barriers, or any voter unable to go to his or her polling place because of conditions resulting in his or her absence from the precinct on election day may request in a written statement, signed under penalty of perjury, that a ballot be delivered to him or her. The ballot shall be delivered by the elections official to the voter or any authorized representative of the voter who presents the written statement to the official.

The voter shall mark the ballot, place it in the identification envelope, fill out and sign the envelope and return the ballot, personally or through the authorized representative, to either the elections official or any polling place within the jurisdiction.

All voted absentee ballots must be received by the City Clerk or by the precinct board at any polling place in the City of Grass Valley by no later than the close of the polls at 8:00 p.m. on May 2, 1989.

185-AV-2

end of file

NOTICE

If you find that for any reason you will be unable to vote in person on election
day, promptly complete and sign the enclosed application for absent voter's ballot
and mail in stamped envelope or return in person to David A. Breninger, City
Administrator/City Clerk, Grass Valley City Hall, 125 East Main Street, Grass
Valley, California 95945 - Telephone (916) 273-2203. Your application may be
submitted not more than 29 days before the day of election but must reach the
Office of the City Clerk not less than 7 days before the day of election.

end of file

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 14, 1989

Schelley Kurle
Production Assistant
Western Elections
P.O. Box 8900
Stockton, CA  95208

Re:  Letter No. 89-099

Dear Mr. Kurle:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on February 10, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John Wallace an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329.)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel
